creatives.headline: One small light. One long night.
creatives.caption: Luma is scared of the dark. Tonight she's the only light her swarm has. Bring the kid who still checks under the bed; this one's for them. In cinemas this summer.

creatives.headline: Nightlights sold separately.
creatives.caption: A film about being scared of the dark, for everyone who ever was. Family tickets at participating cinemas this summer.

creatives.headline: He can imitate anyone. Except himself.
creatives.caption: The Deep End is a comedy about the hardest stroke there is: being yourself. In cinemas this autumn.

creatives.headline: Warning: contains ink.
creatives.caption: The funniest thing in the pool this autumn. Bring the whole lane.

creatives.headline: Two stubborn robots. One junkyard.
creatives.caption: Wrench and Rivet inherit the same scrap heap this spring. Sparks, obviously, fly. Matinee family bundles at participating cinemas.

creatives.headline: For everyone who fixes what others throw away.
creatives.caption: A love letter to repair, one bolt at a time. Wrench & Rivet, in cinemas this spring.

creatives.headline: One thousand cranes. One promise.
creatives.caption: Paper Moon opens this winter. Bring someone who taught you something with their hands.

creatives.headline: You were never really afraid of the dark.
creatives.caption: You were afraid of being the only light. Luma understands. This summer, on the big screen. Evening showtimes from opening night.

creatives.headline: Fold. Repeat. Remember.
creatives.caption: It takes four minutes to fold a paper crane properly. Paper Moon took four years. This winter, at the kitchen table of the big screen.
